creator: lightside@gmx.com
text:
Perhaps, I understood, why Conrad E. Meyer replied in comment #2 this way.
The libelf.so library, installed by devel/elfutils port, somehow correlates with ports-mgmt/pkg port, which broke /usr/sbin/pkg command:
/usr/local/lib/libelf.so.1: version FBSD_1.0 required by /usr/local/lib/libpkg.so.3 not found

And even if ports-mgmt/pkg port built again, the stage-qa gives following messages:
Error: /usr/local/lib/libpkg.so.3.0.0 is linked to /usr/local/lib/libelf.so.1 that does not belong to any package
Error: /usr/local/sbin/pkg is linked to /usr/local/lib/libpkg.so.3 that does not belong to any package

Since, the ports-mgmt/pkg is dependency for all current ports, as well as used for package management, this issue is not easy to solve, I guess. Perhaps, this PR should be closed, because of this. I don't know the situation on FreeBSD 10.3 or 11.
